title: Data Types
description: Explore C++ primitive data types, sizing in memory, and the difference between single and double-precision floats in game engines like Unreal Engine.
learningObjectives: Identify different primitive types (int, float, double, bool, char)
Understand memory storage sizes for primitives on modern 64-bit hardware
Explain float vs double precision difference in modern UE5 engines (Large World Coordinates)

sections.title: Primitive Data Types and Sizing
sections.content:
C++ is a strongly typed language, which means every variable must have a declared type. Primitive types represent the most basic building blocks of data storage:

1. `int` (typically 4 bytes): Stores integers.
2. `float` (typically 4 bytes): Stores single-precision floating-point numbers.
3. `double` (typically 8 bytes): Stores double-precision floating-point numbers.
4. `bool` (typically 1 byte): Stores true or false.
5. `char` (typically 1 byte): Stores single character tokens.

On modern 64-bit operating systems, data sizing is crucial for performance and CPU cache alignment.

sections.title: Unreal Engine 5 Large World Coordinates (LWC)
